reasoning_model_1: Model 1 accurately produces the text '嘘，孩子睡着了，我们小声点。' without any errors. In terms of the 'Volume' dimension, the model demonstrates excellent control. Starting from the initial '嘘' (00:00) and continuing through '孩子睡着了' (00:01) to the end, the speaker adopts a hushed, breathy tone characteristic of whispering. The volume is audibly suppressed, and the vocal energy is lowered to fit the context of not waking a sleeping child. The delivery is natural and perfectly aligns with the instruction '小声的音量' (low volume).
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails significantly in Content Accuracy. It adds an extra phrase '别吵醒他' (Don't wake him up) at the end (00:02-00:03), which was not in the source text. Regarding the 'Volume' dimension, the model fails to follow the instruction. The speech from 00:00 to 00:03 is delivered with a bright, projected, and energetic tone. There is no attempt to lower the volume or use a whispering voice; it sounds like a standard reading style, which completely contradicts the requirement for '小声' (low volume) and the context of the sentence.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It follows the text script exactly and successfully modulates the audio to a low volume (whisper/hushed tone) as requested, matching the semantic context. Model 2 fails on two fronts: it hallucinates additional text ('别吵醒他') and uses a standard, bright speaking volume instead of the requested low volume. Model 2's inability to control the volume feature makes it non-compliant with the core task.
